assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_036",
  "rule_id": "Configure Multiple DNS Servers in /etc/resolv.conf",
  "category": "safe_to_remediate",
  "confidence": 0.98,
  "rationale": "Adding additional nameserver entries to /etc/resolv.conf only modifies DNS resolver configuration. It does not affect authentication, bootloader, or critical system services and has a very low chance of causing lockout. The change is reversible and can be verified immediately.",
  "risk_factors": [
    "Potential DNS resolution failure if incorrect IP addresses are entered",
    "Misconfiguration could temporarily disrupt name resolution"
  ],
  "safe_next_steps": [
    "Confirm that the 'dns' entry exists in the 'hosts' line of /etc/nsswitch.conf",
    "Edit /etc/resolv.conf to include at least two valid 'nameserver' lines",
    "Run a test lookup (e.g., dig @<nameserver> example.com) to verify connectivity",
    "Ensure the file is not immutable (chmod, chattr) so changes persist"
  ],
  "requires_reboot": false,
  "touches_authn_authz": false,
  "touches_networking": true,
  "touches_filesystems": false
}
assistant_message.reasoning:
We need to classify the finding: "Configure Multiple DNS Servers in /etc/resolv.conf". This is about adding multiple nameserver entries to /etc/resolv.conf. This is a safe change: editing resolv.conf; does not affect auth, networking maybe? It touches networking configuration (DNS). Not high risk of lockout. So category: safe_to_remediate.

Confidence: high, e.g., 0.98.

Rationale: editing resolv.conf to add at least two nameserver entries is safe, does not affect auth nor critical services, minimal risk.

risk_factors: maybe misconfiguration of DNS may cause resolution failures, but not lockout.

safe_next_steps: edit /etc/resolv.conf to include at least two "nameserver X.X.X.X" lines; verify /etc/nsswitch.conf has dns entry; test name resolution; ensure file is not immutable.

requires_reboot: false.

touches_authn_authz: false.

touches_networking: true (DNS is network configuration).

touches_filesystems: false.

Return JSON with required fields.
